description_long: This bungalow features a low, single-story profile with a broad, gently sloping gable roof. The exterior walls are finished in a light color, likely stucco or painted siding, with a prominent front porch supported by several columns. The roof overhangs the porch, providing shade and shelter, and the entryway is centrally located, flanked by windows. The windows are rectangular and symmetrically arranged, contributing to a balanced facade. The structure sits on a slightly raised foundation, with steps leading up to the porch, and the overall design is compact and inviting, typical of suburban residential neighborhoods.
description: A single-story bungalow with a wide gable roof, central porch, and symmetrically arranged windows, finished in light tones.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, porch with columns, and steps leading up to the door.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the roof and the side windows, with the porch partially visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the bungalow and the gentle slope of the roof, with minimal window detail.
description_view_3: Angled view emphasizing the porch, front steps, and the overall compact footprint of the bungalow.
